draft.introduction: Luke 23:33–49 narrates the crucifixion with details unique to Luke, including Jesus’ prayer for his executioners and dialogue with the repentant criminal. Luke’s passion emphasizes forgiveness and innocence.

translation.text: And when they came to the place that is called The Skull, there they crucified him, and the criminals, one on his right and one on his left. And Jesus said, ‘Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do.’
translation.original_text: καὶ ὅτε ἦλθον ἐπὶ τὸν τόπον τὸν καλούμενον Κρανίον ἐκεῖ ἐσταύρωσαν αὐτόν

translation.text: It was now about the sixth hour, and there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour. And the curtain of the temple was torn in the middle. Then Jesus, calling out with a loud voice, said, ‘Father, into your hands I commit my spirit!’
translation.original_text: καὶ ἦν ἤδη ὡσεὶ ὥρα ἕκτη καὶ σκότος ἐγένετο ἐφ’ ὅλην τὴν γῆν

translator_notes.note: The prayer “Father, forgive them” is present in Sinaiticus but absent from several early manuscripts including Papyrus 75 and Codex Vaticanus. Some scholars argue it was added by a scribe; others that it was removed because early Christians found it difficult to apply universally.
